name: Pu'u Pia Trail
tagline: The locals' Manoa hike — a muddy ridgeline trail with Ko'olau views and a fraction of the Manoa Falls crowd.
parking: Street parking on Alani Dr (park on the valley side facing ocean)
bestTime: Late afternoon for golden light on the Ko'olau Range; avoid after heavy rain (extremely muddy)
talkStory: While tourists pack Manoa Falls, locals who want a real workout head to Pu'u Pia instead. This 2.1-mile out-and-back trail climbs a ridgeline in the back of Manoa Valley with views of Lyon Arboretum, Mount Olympus, the Ko'olau Range, and Waikiki in the distance. It's rated moderately challenging — mostly because Manoa Valley is one of the wettest spots on Oahu and the trail gets extremely muddy and slippery. Wear shoes you don't mind trashing. The trailhead on Alani Drive has no signage and no facilities, which is exactly why it stays uncrowded. Dogs allowed on leash.
